title: Xtra Crumbly Argentinian Kale Empanadas
description: A salty and crumbly crowd-pleaser featuring kale and tadka, perfect for any occasion, this Argentinian dish is egg-free-friendly and baked in a terracotta dish.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional 1 minute.
Add the chopped kale and cook until wilted, about 3 minutes.
Stir in the tadka spice mix, salt, and black pepper.
Roll out the empanada dough and cut into circles.
Place a spoonful of the kale mixture onto the center of each dough circle.
Fold the dough in half and press the edges to seal.
Place the empanadas in a terracotta dish and bake for 25 minutes.
